events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: flanged joints in commercial piping, observed condition is wrong gasket type/material for service. Work is in-progress. Need to evaluate sequence and state what must happen before work continues.

Wrong gasket material for service is a non-compliance/defect. The joint is installed-defective. Work must stop, gaskets must be replaced with correct material per submittal/service requirements before continuing.

Decision: fail (non-compliant). Risk: high (wrong gasket can leak/fail under service). Work stage: in-progress. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 50. Value 0. Confidence high. Order: not needed. Workable: not asked. Findings: wrong gasket type/material for service. Actions: stop work, remove and replace gaskets with approved type per submittal, reinspect. Rationale: gasket must match service media/pressure/temp per ASME PCC-1 and B16.5. References.

    "Reinspect affected joints before pressure testing and continuing work"

ASME PCC-1 and B16.5 require gaskets suitable for the service conditions and compatible with the flange rating. Work cannot continue until the defective gaskets are replaced and verified.",

}
